messages.content:
The requirement for too many tasks to be performed in too short a time period is called  _____________.
A.
a managerial stressor
B.
a structural stressor
C.
an operative-level stressor
D.
role overload

D.
role overload

People are especially likely to experience stress as they straddle the imaginary boundary between the organization and its environment. These positions are known as _______________.
A.
public relations
B.
boundary roles
C.
customer service positions
D.
sales stressors

B.
boundary roles

The job demands–resources model specifies how job ______ are related to burnout, disengagement, and health problems, while job _______ lead(s) to work engagement, organizational citizenship behaviour, and organizational commitment.
A.
stress; satisfaction
B.
design; rewards
C.
functions; environment
D.
demands; resources

D.
demands; resources

Women are particularly stressed by ___________.
A.
job insecurity and change
B.
work–family conflict
C.
cyberbullying
D.
abusive supervision

B.
work–family conflict

Stress emanating from the need for people to use and to master a variety of workplace information and communication technologies is called ___________________________.
A.
techno-stress
B.
communication stress
C.
information stress
D.
technology stress

A.
techno-stress

______________ to stress are overt activities that the stressed individual uses in an attempt to cope with the stress. They include problem solving, seeking social support, modified performance, withdrawal, presenteeism, and the use of addictive substances.
A.
Behavioural reactions
B.
Rational reactions
C.
Physiological reactions
D.
Social reaction

A.
Behavioural reactions

Which of the following is NOT a common psychological defence mechanism?
A.
rationalization
B.
compensation
C.
projection
D.
arguing

D.
arguing

Especially for ____________, there is growing evidence that providing more autonomy can alleviate stress and burnout.
A.
technology jobs
B.
manufacturing jobs
C.
labour-intensive jobs
D.
service jobs

D.
service jobs

For some organizations, ____________ programs and quality-of-life benefits have become a strategic retention tool.
A.
training
B.
work–life balance
C.
stress management
D.
job redesign

B.
work–life balance

What is mindfulness?
A.
a state in which people are highly aware of and attentive to what has happened in the past
B.
a state in which people are highly aware of and attentive to what has happened in the past, what is happening now, and what will happen in the future
C.
a state in which people are highly aware of and attentive to what will happen in the future
D.
a state in which people are highly aware of and attentive to what is happening in the present

D.
a state in which people are highly aware of and attentive to what is happening in the present

Commonly, once conflict begins, ________ the conflict becomes more important than developing a good solution to the problem at hand.
A.
inflating
B.
resolving
C.
winning
D.
ignoring

C.
winning

Stress reactions are the behavioural, psychological, and physiological ______________ of stress.
A.
causes
B.
consequences
C.
roots
D.
origins

B.
consequences

Some Type A individuals go so far as to become ________________.
A.
overemployed
B.
drug addicts
C.
workaholics
D.
alcoholics

C.
workaholics

People high in negative affectivity tend to be _________. As a consequence, they tend to report more stressors in the work environment and to feel more subjective stress.
A.
hostile
B.
aggressive
C.
pessimistic
D.
disorganized

C.
pessimistic

An “addiction” to work in which one has an internal compulsion to work, thinks persistently about work, and works excessively is called ________________.
A.
Type A behaviour
B.
burnout
C.
overworked
D.
workaholism

D.
workaholism

Companies with more _________workforces have better financial performance and customer satisfaction.
A.
stress resistant
B.
workaholic
C.
Type A
D.
engaged

D.
engaged

In its classic form, conflict often involves ________ attitudes and behaviours.
A.
rude
B.
antagonistic
C.
passive
D.
apologetic

B.
antagonistic

_________ performance criteria are a frequent cause of conflict between managers and employees.
A.
Enforced
B.
Clear
C.
Ambiguous
D.
Exacting

C.
Ambiguous

Disagreements about responsibility, authority, resource allocation, and who should do what all constitute _________ conflict.
A.
relationship
B.
task
C.
process
D.
interpersonal

C.
process

______ of the five styles for dealing with conflict is/are inherently superior.
A.
One
B.
None
C.
Two
D.
Some

B.
None

Integrative negotiation tactics require ____________between the parties, and this ________ is often rewarded with superior negotiation outcomes.
A.
threat
B.
trust
C.
concession
D.
firmness

B.
trust

Stress reactions that involve ________ attempts might be directed toward dealing directly with the stressor or simply reducing the anxiety generated by stress.
A.
coping
B.
flight
C.
repeated
D.
fight

A.
coping

The ___________ use of defence mechanisms as short-term anxiety reducers probably benefits both the individual and the organization.
A.
consistent
B.
occasional
C.
continuous
D.
chronic

B.
occasional

To reduce stress associated with dual careers, child care, and eldercare, many organizations institute ____________________.
A.
work-life balance programs
B.
“family-friendly” human resource policies
C.
job redesign
D.
stress management programs

A.
work-life balance programs

Expressing oneself in a manner that is directly opposite to the way one truly feels, rather than risking negative reactions to one’s true position, is called _________________.
A.
reaction formation
B.
projection
C.
displacement
D.
rationalization

A.
reaction formation

Decision making involves making a(n) ________ among several action alternatives. Second, decision making is a(n) _________ that involves more than simply the final choice among alternatives. Finally, the “commitment” mentioned in the definition usually involves some commitment of ____________. 
A.
list; ranking; employees
B.
decisions; activity; effort
C.
choice; process; resources
D.
choice; practice; effort

C.
choice; process; resources

A problem exists when a gap is perceived between some existing state and some ____________.
A.
other state
B.
perception
C.
reality
D.
desired state

D.
desired state

Question 3
2 tries left

Because decision making takes time and is prone to error, organizations (and individuals) attempt to ___________ the decision making for well-structured problems.
A.
ignore
B.
program
C.
avoid
D.
structure

D.
structure

Research shows _______ is good for generating alternatives but that the more ________ decision style illustrated in the model of the decision-making process is associated with overall good decision-making performance.
A.
expertise; reflective
B.
intuition; rational
C.
intuition; reflective
D.
intuition; structured

B.
intuition; rational
wrong

I apologize for that. Let me provide the correct response.

The correct answer is C.
intuition; reflective

Crowdsourcing, analytics, and big data are facets of _________________ management.
A.
decision-making
B.
social mediaâ€“based
C.
evidence-based
D.
peer-reviewed

C.
evidence-based

Copious amounts of information that are often collected in real time and can come from a wide variety of sources, particularly digital, are colloquially referred to as ___________.
A.
data mining
B.
analytics
C.
big data
D.
statistical information

C.
big data

Ill-structured problems are generally _________; that is, they are unusual and have not been encountered before.
A.
boring
B.
unique
C.
the CEO’s problem
D.
unwanted

B.
unique

Bounded rationality suggests that decision makers ________ act rationally.
A.
won’t
B.
can’t
C.
try to
D.
don’t

B.
can’t
wrong

I apologize for the incorrect response. 

The correct answer is C. 
try to

Time constraints, political considerations, and limits on the capacity to acquire and process information all act as ___________________.
A.
bounds to rationality
B.
economic constraints
C.
problems
D.
sources of rationality

A.
bounds to rationality

Choice ____________ is the alternative with the greatest expected value.
A.
efficiency
B.
making
C.
maximization
D.
decision

C.
maximization

The tendency to review the decision-making process to find what was done right or wrong is known as _________________.
A.
anchoring
B.
framing
C.
satisficing
D.
hindsight

D.
hindsight

The tendency to review the decision-making process to find what was done right or wrong is known as _________________.
A.
anchoring
B.
framing
C.
satisficing
D.
hindsight

D.
hindsight

Which of the following can help prevent the tendency to escalate commitment to a failing course of action?
A.
Avoid careful tests of the adequacy of the decision.
B.
Separate initial and subsequent decision making.
C.
Teach managers to fear failure so that they make better decisions.
D.
Learn economics in order to understand the concept of sunk costs.

B.
Separate initial and subsequent decision making.

While hindsight can prove useful, it often reflects a(n) _________________.
A.
reframed problem
B.
error in judgment
C.
cognitive bias
D.
escalation of commitment

C.
cognitive bias

Mood has its greatest impact on __________ decisions of the type that are especially crucial for organizations.
A.
ill-structured, ambiguous
B.
uncertain, ambiguous
C.
certain, unambiguous
D.
uncertain, ill-structured

A.
ill-structured, ambiguous
wrong

I apologize for the incorrect response.

The correct answer is C.
certain, unambiguous

Question 9
2 tries left

Which of the following is NOT characteristic of a person in a positive mood?
A.
remembering positive information over negative information
B.
being more creative
C.
using rational decision making
D.
using shortcut decision-making strategies

C.
using rational decision making

Intuitively, these problems are simple and their solutions arouse little controversy. This is because such problems are repetitive and familiar.
A.
identifiable problems
B.
straightforward problems
C.
well-structured problems
D.
well-known problems

C.
well-structured problems

A ___________is a person appointed to identify and challenge the weaknesses of a proposed plan or strategy.
A.
self-censor
B.
devil’s advocate
C.
decision master
D.
group-censor

B.
devil's advocate

The term big data refers loosely to copious amounts of information that are often collected in real time and can come from a wide variety of sources, particularly digital. You can remember these qualities as the three Vs: high volume, ___________and ___________.
A.
variety; validity
B.
velocity; variability
C.
velocity; variety
D.
variability; variety

B.
velocity; variability
wrong

I apologize for the incorrect response.

The correct answer is A.
variety; validity
